description_long: This awning features a large, pitched roof with a dark, shingle-style covering, supported by a sturdy wooden frame. The roof is composed of uniformly arranged rectangular shingles in varying shades of brown, giving it a realistic and weather-resistant appearance. Four vertical wooden posts support the structure at each corner, connected by horizontal beams for added stability. The underside of the roof reveals a simple truss system, providing both structural integrity and an open, airy feel beneath the canopy. The edges of the roof are straight and clean, with no decorative trim or valance, emphasizing a functional and utilitarian design. This type of awning is commonly found in parks, public gathering spaces, or as a shelter in urban recreational areas.
description: A large, pitched-roof awning with dark shingle tiles and a robust wooden post-and-beam frame. The design is functional, with straight edges and no decorative trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched roof, shingle pattern, and the open truss structure beneath, supported by four wooden posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the broad expanse of the shingled roof and the straight, clean edge with visible support posts.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the pitched roof and open truss system clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the wide shingled roof and the straight edge, with support posts visible.
attribute_mounting_location: freestanding on flat ground or pavement
attribute_canopy_pattern: rectangular shingle pattern in alternating brown tones
attribute_edge_trim_style: straight edge with no decorative trim
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame with four vertical posts and horizontal beams
